note: FROZEN contract for the PURE resolveProject(encodedDir, {knownRoots, aliases}). The Claude `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>` dir name maps every `/` and `.` to `-` and preserves literal hyphens, so it is NOT reversible (a sibling repo `yard-ops-extra` and a nested dir `yard-ops/extra` encode identically). Resolution is therefore a forward, segment-boundary longest-prefix match: encode each knownRoot the same way; an instance dir matches a root if it equals it or continues it at a `-` boundary; the longest match wins; 0 matches -> __unattributed__ (fail closed). `aliases` map a worktree/old-name absolute path to its canonical git-toplevel root. Pins LABEL correctness (which token reconciliation cannot). Cases authored 2026-06-29 (Phase 0-B); encodings verified against the real ~/.claude/projects scheme.

cases.name: nested cwd under a repo -> the enclosing repo
sibling-prefix: a run IN yard-ops resolves to yard-ops, not yard-ops-extra
sibling-prefix: a run IN yard-ops-extra resolves to yard-ops-extra (longest match wins, not yard-ops)
nested under yard-ops with a sibling yard-ops-extra present -> still the enclosing yard-ops
alias hit: a worktree cwd resolves to its canonical root's key+label
unresolvable: a cwd under no known root -> __unattributed__ (fail closed)
home-dir catch-all is NOT a root -> a bare home cwd is unattributed (the cwd-as-project unsoundness)
